esv_version: Oh, taste and see that the LORD is good! Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him!
niv_version: Taste and see that the LORD is good; blessed is the one who takes refuge in him.
reflection: When life feels uncertain or heavy, God invites you to experience His goodness for yourself. Taking refuge in Him brings blessing and a quiet confidence that He is better than anything else you could turn to.
live_it_out:
1. Pause today and intentionally "taste" one evidence of God's goodness.
2. Run to Him as your refuge in one moment of worry.
3. Share one way you've seen His goodness with a friend.

esv_version: The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the righteous man runs into it and is safe.
niv_version: The name of the LORD is a fortified tower; the righteous run to it and are safe.
reflection: When everything feels unstable, the name of the Lord is a strong, secure tower you can run into. Safety and peace are found simply by calling on Him.
live_it_out:
1. When you feel overwhelmed, run to God's name in prayer.
2. Speak His name over one area of stress today.
3. Be a safe place for someone else who needs refuge.

esv_version: You keep him in perfect peace whose mind is stayed on you, because he trusts in you.
niv_version: You will keep in perfect peace those whose minds are steadfast, because they trust in you.
reflection: A racing mind and constant anxiety are common in these years, but God offers perfect peace to the mind that stays focused on Him. Trust is the key that unlocks it.
live_it_out:
1. When your mind starts racing, gently bring it back to God.
2. Practice trusting Him with one specific worry today.
3. Thank Him for the peace that comes when you stay focused on Him.

esv_version: Call to me and I will answer you, and will tell you great and hidden things that you have not known.
niv_version: Call to me and I will answer you and tell you great and unsearchable things you do not know.
reflection: God doesn’t hide from your questions—He invites them. When you call on Him, He answers and reveals things your heart needs to know.
live_it_out:
1. Bring one honest question or hidden worry to God in prayer today.
2. Listen quietly for His answer instead of rushing to figure it out alone.
3. Journal what He reveals, even if it’s just a sense of peace.

esv_version: The LORD is good to those who wait for him, to the soul who seeks him.
niv_version: The LORD is good to those whose hope is in him, to the one who seeks him.
reflection: Waiting can feel like wasted time, but God is actively good to those who wait with hope and seek Him. Your waiting is never empty when it’s filled with seeking.
live_it_out:
1. Choose to wait with hope in one area where you’re impatient today.
2. Seek God intentionally while you wait instead of just enduring it.
3. Remind yourself “The Lord is good to those who wait for Him.”

esv_version: God, the Lord, is my strength; he makes my feet like the deer’s; he makes me tread on my high places.
niv_version: The Sovereign LORD is my strength; he makes my feet like the feet of a deer, he enables me to tread on the heights.
reflection: God doesn’t just give you strength—He makes you sure-footed even on the high, difficult places of life. You can walk confidently because He enables you.
live_it_out:
1. When a challenge feels like “high ground,” ask God to make your feet like the deer’s.
2. Take one step today on something that feels unsteady, trusting His strength.
3. Thank Him for being the One who enables you to tread on the heights.

esv_version: The LORD your God is in your midst, a mighty one who will save; he will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.
niv_version: The LORD your God is with you, the Mighty Warrior who saves. He will take great delight in you; in his love he will no longer rebuke you, but will rejoice over you with singing.
reflection: God is right in the middle of your life—a mighty Savior who delights in you and sings over you with joy. His love quiets every fear.
live_it_out:
1. Let God quiet you with His love when you feel anxious.
2. Picture Him rejoicing over you with singing today.
3. Live like someone who is deeply delighted in by God.

esv_version: Yet now be strong, O Zerubbabel, declares the LORD. Be strong, O Joshua, son of Jehozadak, the high priest. Be strong, all you people of the land, declares the LORD. Work, for I am with you.
niv_version: But now be strong, Zerubbabel,’ declares the LORD. ‘Be strong, Joshua son of Jozadak, the high priest. Be strong, all you people of the land,’ declares the LORD, ‘and work. For I am with you.’
reflection: God’s call to be strong isn’t a solo effort. He says “be strong…and work” because He is right there with you, empowering every step.
live_it_out:
1. Hear God saying “Be strong” over one area where you feel weak.
2. Take one practical step of work today, knowing He is with you.
3. Encourage someone else with the same promise.

esv_version: Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.
niv_version: Not by might nor by power, but by my Spirit,’ says the LORD Almighty.
reflection: You don’t have to rely on your own strength or cleverness. God’s Spirit is the real power that accomplishes what matters most.
live_it_out:
1. In a task that feels too big, depend on the Holy Spirit instead of your own might.
2. Pray “Not by my power, but by Your Spirit” before you begin.
3. Watch for God’s Spirit at work in ways you couldn’t manufacture.

esv_version: Bring the full tithe into the storehouse... and thereby put me to the test, says the LORD of hosts, if I will not open the windows of heaven for you and pour down for you a blessing until there is no more need.
niv_version: Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse... and test me in this,” says the LORD Almighty, “and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.
reflection: God actually invites you to test Him in the area of generosity. When you trust Him with what you have, He opens the floodgates of blessing.
live_it_out:
1. Give generously (time, money, kindness) as an act of trust today.
2. Quietly test God by expecting His blessing in return.
3. Watch for the floodgates of heaven in unexpected places.

esv_version: Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
niv_version: Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
reflection: Hearing Jesus is good, but doing what He says is what makes your life steady and storm-proof. Obedience builds on rock.
live_it_out:
1. Pick one thing Jesus said and put it into practice today.
2. Build your day on the rock of obedience instead of feelings.
3. Thank Him that doing His words makes you wise.

esv_version: And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.
niv_version: Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.
reflection: Jesus doesn’t ask for part of you—He wants all of you. Loving God with your whole heart, soul, mind, and strength brings every part of your life into alignment.
live_it_out:
1. Tell God you want to love Him with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength.
2. Give Him one area you’ve been holding back.
3. Let that full love shape how you spend your day.

esv_version: Why, even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not; you are of more value than many sparrows.
niv_version: Indeed, the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Don’t be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
reflection: God knows every detail about you—even the number of hairs on your head. You are deeply known and highly valued; fear has no place.
live_it_out:
1. When fear or insecurity rises, remember you are known and valued.
2. Thank God for caring about the smallest details of your life.
3. Treat someone else like they are highly valued today.

esv_version: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life. He does not come into judgment, but has passed from death to life.
niv_version: Very truly I tell you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life and will not be judged but has crossed over from death to life.
reflection: Eternal life isn’t something you wait for—it begins the moment you hear and believe. You have already crossed from death to life.
live_it_out:
1. Thank Jesus that you have already passed from death to life.
2. Live today like someone who is no longer under judgment.
3. Share this good news with a friend who needs hope.

esv_version: For in him we live and move and have our being.
niv_version: For in him we live and move and have our being.
reflection: You are not independent—every breath, every move, every part of your existence is held in God. You live, move, and have your being in Him.
live_it_out:
1. Throughout the day, quietly remind yourself “In Him I live and move and have my being.”
2. Depend on God for strength in one ordinary moment.
3. Thank Him that your whole life is held in Him.

esv_version: For you did not receive the spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you have received the Spirit of adoption as sons, by whom we cry, “Abba! Father!”
niv_version: The Spirit you received does not make you slaves, so that you live in fear again; rather, the Spirit you received brought about your adoption to sonship. And by him we cry, “Abba, Father.”
reflection: You are not a slave to fear anymore. You have been adopted as God’s own child and can cry out “Abba, Father” with confidence and intimacy.
live_it_out:
1. When fear tries to pull you back, remember you have the Spirit of adoption.
2. Call God “Abba, Father” in prayer today.
3. Live like a loved son or daughter instead of a fearful slave.

esv_version: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
niv_version: Now you are the body of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it.
reflection: You are not an extra or an outsider—you are an essential part of the body of Christ. Every role matters, and you belong.
live_it_out:
1. Remember today that you are a needed part of Christ’s body.
2. Use your gifts to serve someone in the body.
3. Thank God that you belong and are valued.

esv_version: But we have this treasure in jars of clay, to show that the surpassing power belongs to God and not to us.
niv_version: But we have this treasure in jars of clay to show that this all-surpassing power is from God and not from us.
reflection: You don’t have to be impressive or unbreakable. God puts His treasure in ordinary jars of clay so everyone can see that the power is from Him, not you.
live_it_out:
1. Embrace your “jar of clay” weaknesses today instead of hiding them.
2. Let God’s surpassing power show through in one area.
3. Give Him glory when people notice something good in you.

esv_version: I have been crucified with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me.
niv_version: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me.
reflection: Your old self is gone. Christ now lives in you—this is the most freeing identity shift you can experience.
live_it_out:
1. Remind yourself throughout the day “Christ lives in me.”
2. Let His life influence one decision or conversation.
3. Rest in the freedom that it’s no longer just you trying.

esv_version: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them.
niv_version: For we are God’s handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.
reflection: You are God’s masterpiece, hand-crafted with purpose. He has already prepared good works for you to walk in—your life matters.
live_it_out:
1. Thank God that you are His workmanship.
2. Look for one good work He prepared for you today.
3. Walk in it with confidence.

esv_version: Not that I have already obtained this or am already perfect, but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own.
niv_version: Not that I have already obtained all this, or have already arrived at my goal, but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me.
reflection: You don’t have to be perfect or have arrived. You can keep pressing on because Christ has already taken hold of you.
live_it_out:
1. Press on in one area where you feel you haven’t arrived yet.
2. Remember that Christ has taken hold of you.
3. Celebrate progress instead of perfection.

esv_version: Put on then, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, compassionate hearts, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience.
niv_version: Therefore, as God’s chosen people, holy and dearly loved, clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience.
reflection: You are already chosen, holy, and dearly loved. Now you can “put on” compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience like clothes that fit who you really are.
live_it_out:
1. Choose one quality (compassion, kindness, etc.) to “put on” today.
2. Remember you are dearly loved while you wear it.
3. Let these qualities flow naturally because of who you are in Christ.

esv_version: He who calls you is faithful; he will surely do it.
niv_version: The one who calls you is faithful, and he will do it.
reflection: God is the One who called you, and He is faithful. Whatever He has started in your life, He will surely complete.
live_it_out:
1. Remind yourself today “The One who called me is faithful.”
2. Trust Him to do what He has promised in your life.
3. Rest in the assurance that He will finish what He started.

esv_version: So flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
niv_version: Flee the evil desires of youth and pursue righteousness, faith, love and peace, along with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
reflection: You’re not meant to fight every battle alone. Flee what pulls you down and pursue what’s good—together with others who love the Lord with pure hearts.
live_it_out:
1. Actively flee one youthful passion that’s been tempting you.
2. Pursue righteousness, faith, love, or peace in a practical way today.
3. Reach out to a friend who calls on the Lord and encourage each other.

esv_version: He saved us, not because of works done by us in righteousness, but according to his own mercy, by the washing of regeneration and renewal of the Holy Spirit.
niv_version: He saved us, not because of righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He saved us through the washing of rebirth and renewal by the Holy Spirit.
reflection: Your salvation has never depended on being good enough. It’s all mercy, rebirth, and the Holy Spirit’s renewing work—free and complete.
live_it_out:
1. Rest in God’s mercy today instead of trying to earn His approval.
2. Ask the Holy Spirit to renew one tired part of you.
3. Share this freeing truth with someone who feels they have to perform.

esv_version: Strive for peace with everyone, and for the holiness without which no one will see the Lord.
niv_version: Make every effort to live in peace with everyone and to be holy; without holiness no one will see the Lord.
reflection: Peace with others and personal holiness matter deeply because they affect how clearly you see God. Both are worth striving for with His help.
live_it_out:
1. Make every effort toward peace in one relationship today.
2. Pursue holiness in one small, practical choice.
3. Ask God to help you see Him more clearly through these efforts.

esv_version: And a harvest of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
niv_version: Peacemakers who sow in peace reap a harvest of righteousness.
reflection: Being a peacemaker isn’t passive—it’s active sowing that produces a harvest of righteousness. Your efforts to bring peace matter more than you know.
live_it_out:
1. Look for one opportunity to make peace today instead of escalating conflict.
2. Sow peace in your words or actions.
3. Trust that a harvest of righteousness is coming from it.

esv_version: But in your hearts honor Christ the Lord as holy, always being prepared to make a defense to anyone who asks you for a reason for the hope that is in you; yet do it with gentleness and respect.
niv_version: But in your hearts revere Christ as Lord. Always be prepared to give an answer to everyone who asks you to give the reason for the hope that you have. But do this with gentleness and respect.
reflection: You don’t have to be pushy or preachy. When your heart honors Christ as Lord, you’re naturally ready to share the hope inside you—with gentleness and respect.
live_it_out:
1. Quietly honor Christ as Lord in your heart right now.
2. Be ready with a gentle, respectful answer if someone asks about your hope.
3. Pray for one person who might ask you about your faith soon.

esv_version: But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory both now and to the day of eternity.
niv_version: But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be glory both now and forever! Amen.
reflection: Growth isn’t a one-time event—it’s a lifelong journey in grace and in knowing Jesus more. Every step brings glory to Him, now and forever.
live_it_out:
1. Choose one way to grow in grace or knowledge of Jesus today.
2. Give Him glory for any growth you’ve already seen.
3. Keep going even when growth feels slow.

esv_version: And now, little children, abide in him, so that when he appears we may have confidence and not shrink from him in shame at his coming.
niv_version: And now, dear children, continue in him, so that when he appears we may be confident and unashamed before him at his coming.
reflection: Abiding in Jesus isn’t complicated—it’s daily closeness that builds confidence instead of shame when He returns. That relationship is what matters most.
live_it_out:
1. Choose to abide in Jesus today through simple conversation with Him.
2. Ask Him to help you live with confidence in His return.
3. Let that future hope shape one choice you make.

esv_version: And he who was seated on the throne said, “Behold, I am making all things new.”
niv_version: He who was seated on the throne said, “I am making everything new!”
reflection: No matter how broken or ordinary your story feels, the One on the throne is making all things new. Your life is part of His beautiful renewal project.
live_it_out:
1. Look at one area that feels old or broken and declare “He is making it new.”
2. Cooperate with God’s renewal in one small way today.
3. Live with hope because the King is making everything new.
